title: pilosella kalksburgensis subsp. lepteilema
intro:
Pilosella kalksburgensis subsp. lepteilema, commonly known as a subspecies of the plant Pilosella kalksburgensis, is a rare and lesser-known species of plant in the dandelion family. It is a type of flowering plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Pilosella kalksburgensis subsp. lepteilema include sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to have therapeutic actions such as inhibiting the growth of cancer cells and reducing inflammation.
